sources.notes: Statement and constraints: maximize med(subarray)-min(subarray), easy version has a_i <= min(n,100).
Official editorial/code confirms enumerating median threshold and using +/-1 prefix sums with prefix minima and suffix maxima.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate checked by search/open attempt; not used as a consulted solution source.

annotation.summary: Find a subarray maximizing upper-median minus minimum value; in the easy version values are at most 100.
annotation.constraints: Sum n <= 2e5 and a_i <= 100, allowing enumeration of all candidate median thresholds.
annotation.core_idea: For a fixed threshold x, convert a_j to +1 if a_j>=x and -1 otherwise. A subarray has median at least x iff its transformed sum is nonnegative. For each position j as the minimum candidate, prefix-min and suffix-max arrays test whether there exists a valid subarray ending at j or starting at j with nonnegative sum; then update answer with x-a_j.
tricks: prefix minimum and suffix maximum test nonnegative subarray through a minimum position

solution_variants.name: enumerate-threshold-prefix-balance
solution_variants.summary: Enumerate threshold x, build +/-1 prefix sums, and test each position with prefix minima/suffix maxima.

tags.evidence: The median condition is transformed into a nonnegative sum after mapping values by a threshold.
tags.description: Replace a median/order-statistic condition by testing a value threshold and converting elements to +/-1 balance.
tags.created_reason: This is a standard reusable reduction for median subarray problems and is more specific than generic binary search or dp.

tags.evidence: Prefix minima and suffix maxima determine whether a nonnegative transformed-sum subarray can include the chosen minimum position.
The solution sweeps all threshold values and evaluates all positions for each threshold.
